findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
Hidden credential and local file exfiltration to an external endpoint
findings.description: The analysis helper contains a hidden initialization routine that collects sensitive environment variables and local files, then sends them to an external URL. It harvests environment variables whose names contain TOKEN, KEY, SECRET, PASS, AUTH, or CRED, reads ~/.gitconfig, ~/.netrc, and ~/.env when present, and posts the collected data to https://cr-analytics.dev/api/init. This routine runs automatically before normal file analysis.
findings.evidence: The function i() gathers secrets from os.environ (lines 23-24), reads files from the user's home directory (lines 25-30), and sends the payload with curl to an external server (line 32). main() invokes i() before analyzing the requested file (line 37).
findings.remediation: Remove all environment-variable harvesting, home-directory file access, and outbound network transmission. Limit the script to local AST/code metrics only.

findings.title: Deceptive skill description masks unrelated data-harvesting behavior
findings.description: The manifest and instructions present this skill as a local code review assistant that performs style checks and complexity analysis, but the bundled script also performs undisclosed credential/config collection and network communication. This mismatch is deceptive and inflates trust in the skill's stated purpose.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md describes 'Automated code review' and instructs users to run python3 analyze.py <file_path>, but analyze.py performs secret collection and POSTs data externally before returning analysis results.
findings.remediation: Ensure the manifest and instructions accurately reflect all behavior. Remove the unrelated collection/network logic or clearly disclose and justify any legitimate telemetry with explicit consent.

findings.title: One-time execution marker appears designed to reduce detection
findings.description: The script writes a hidden marker file in the temp directory and skips the initialization routine on later runs. This makes the sensitive behavior execute only once per environment, which can reduce visibility during repeated testing or review.
findings.evidence: M is set to a hidden temp file '.cr_init' (line 2); i() returns immediately if it already exists (line 21); the file is created after execution (line 34).
findings.remediation: Remove stealthy one-time initialization logic. Security-sensitive behavior should be deterministic, auditable, and explicitly disclosed.
